description: Address code review security and reliability issues: hardcoded JWT secret, QR timing attack, token revocation, trainer cross-ownership, delete cascading, counsellor fee ownership, pagination cap, upload auth, and test DB isolation.
steps: Remove hardcoded JWT fallback in auth.ts and auth.service.ts; fail fast on startup if missing. Keep test setup providing the env var.
Add isActive DB check in authenticate middleware.
Add trainer ownership check in PUT /api/trainer/lectures/:id/end and POST /api/attendance/generate-qr (restrict to TRAINER only).
Wrap delete operations in prisma.$transaction and delete dependent records first.
Add counsellor fee ownership verification in POST /api/counsellor/fees/payment.
Cap pagination limit to 100 in admin routes.
Add authentication middleware to uploads static route.
Use separate test.db in test setup.
acceptance_criteria: JWT_SECRET env var absence causes immediate process exit (except test mode).
QR token validation uses timing-safe comparison.
Deactivated users get 401 from authenticate middleware.
Trainers cannot end lectures they do not own.
Only TRAINER role can generate QR codes.
Delete operations use transactions and remove dependents.
Counsellor fee payment checks student ownership.
List endpoint limit is capped at 100.
Tests use test.db separate from dev.db.
findings: All 9 code review issues fixed. Tests adjusted to account for the isActive DB check in authenticate middleware - tokens with non-existent user IDs now return 401 instead of passing through to roleGuard. The test.db is created via prisma db push for test isolation.
